seoTitle: Kennedy vs Kensington Grove — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kennedy and Kensington Grove. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Kennedy vs Kensington Grove: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kennedy and Kensington Grove.
heading: Kennedy vs Kensington Grove — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kennedy, Queensland and Kensington Grove, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kennedy has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Kensington Grove (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kennedy or Kensington Grove safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kennedy has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Kensington Grove scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kennedy scores 27/100 for bushfire risk. Kensington Grove scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kennedy or Kensington Grove?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kennedy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
